design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Switchback experiment carryover identification by de Bruijn schedule certificate. Pre-anchor: closest published anchors are switchback/time-series experiment papers on carryover and design balance, plus classical crossover-design carryover aliasing; closest banked proposals are eid_policy_commutator_phase_v1 and panel_approx_nbhd_interference_v1, neither of which contains a schedule-history incidence frontier. Our theorem is not do-calculus, g-formula, support coverage, LP, or a generic rank slogan: it gives a finite nonroutine schedule object. For lag length L=2, the cyclic de Bruijn schedule 0011 has ordered treatment histories 00,01,11,10 exactly once, so the four-history incidence matrix is the 4 by 4 identity after ordering histories as 00,01,11,10. The alternating schedule 0101 observes only histories 01 and 10, and the written non-equivalence certificate is delta=(1,0,-1,0): adding delta to the four history means changes the policy contrast between all-zero and all-one regimes but leaves every alternating observed history mean unchanged. Conjecture kernel: for binary switchback designs with unknown L-lag history effects and no parametric smoothing, exact identification of all L-history policy means is possible iff the cyclic history-incidence matrix has full column rank; the minimum-period exact design has T=2^L and is attained by a de Bruijn cycle. Why non-trivial: the object is a concrete incidence/certificate table and a minimality phase T=2^L derived from primitive histories, not an estimator variance or adjustment formula. Why promising: it gives a sharp design frontier for carryover-aware switchback experiments and an explicit alias witness explaining why common alternating schedules cannot identify long-run policy contrasts. Reject if this collapses to standard crossover-design balance, definition-unfold rank algebra, weak-ID/rank perturbation, or a theorem that merely applies de Bruijn sequences to a new causal vocabulary without a comparator-facing switchback implication.

proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue artefacts remain q1_spectral_phase_transition/p1_markov, eid_policy_commutator_phase/v1, and panel_approx_nbhd_interference/v1. The revision preserves the gap: none states a target-specific switchback schedule audit that returns either an observed-mean formula or a policy-relevant alias vector, and none contains the unknown-influence-period sustained-contrast frontier.","published_axis":"The revision no longer claims the order-2 de Bruijn table as novel, since AguirreMattarMagisWeinberg2011 already supplies compact de Bruijn counterbalance. The published gap is now comparator-facing: BojinovSimchiLeviZhao2023 assumes a carryover order for design optimization, MissaultMasoero2025 studies underestimated carryover, and OmanSeiden1988 studies residual balance, while the revised kernel supplies a deterministic target-specific alias/identification audit for switchback schedule means."}
seed_list: Characterize exact identification of every binary L-history switchback policy mean by full column rank of the cyclic schedule-history incidence matrix, with de Bruijn cycles attaining the 2^L minimum period.
Give a finite alias-certificate algorithm that returns either a full-rank schedule-history table or a null vector changing a sustained-regime contrast, with 0011 versus 0101 worked out for L=2.
Separate classical residual balance from full L-history identification by constructing schedules that satisfy first-order crossover balance but have rank-deficient higher-order history incidence.
Characterize multi-unit switchback exact identification by the aggregate unit-period history-incidence matrix and determine when units can shorten each individual period below 2^L.
Define the sustained-regime contrast row-space frontier: Delta_L is identified iff e_{1^L}-e_{0^L} lies in the row span of A(w,L), even when the full history vector is not identified.
Construct a de Bruijn-corrected switchback design audit that replaces alternating schedules when the alias vector changes the all-zero versus all-one policy contrast.
Give a critical-lag certificate for influence-period misspecification: the first L at which a schedule loses full history-rank determines when guessed carryover bounds can bias sustained contrasts.
Bridge geometric-mixing switchbacks and finite-memory switchbacks by proving when burn-in block designs induce a full-rank finite-history submatrix.
Extend de Bruijn incidence to regular balanced multi-item switchbacks through a matching condition on item-time histories and a lower bound on total observed histories.
Produce higher-order crossover alias witnesses showing that residual-effect balance can hold while primitive L-bit history means remain unidentified.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize exact identification of every binary L-history switchback policy mean by full column rank of the cyclic schedule-history incidence matrix, with de Bruijn cycles attaining the 2^L minimum period.

anchor_paper.author: Bojinov, Simchi-Levi, and Zhao
anchor_paper.claim: Optimal switchback design under carryover-order assumptions, not full history-table identification.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: under a range of different assumptions on the order of the carryover effect

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: T=2 to general L is stronger here because the new object is a schedule-history incidence certificate with alias side.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from de Bruijn counterbalancing because every word appears once.
seed_details.rebuttal: Aguirre et al. counterbalance stimuli; they do not state the causal observation map, row-space iff, or switchback alias certificate.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not AguirreMattarMagisWeinberg2011 because the contribution is the causal exact-ID iff and alias frontier, not compact sequence generation.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: rank is used to construct a focal incidence frontier and explicit policy-contrast aliases, not as a slogan.

adversarial_referee.instance: May read as de Bruijn vocabulary only.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The focal object is A(w,L) plus alias certificates for causal policy means.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity could be left as rank-nullity.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Necessity is tied to nonparametric primitive histories and policy-relevant null vectors.

adversarial_referee.instance: Crossover literature may already know the balance boundary.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Residual balance differs from full ordered L-bit history incidence.

seed_details.one_line: Give a finite alias-certificate algorithm that returns either a full-rank schedule-history table or a null vector changing a sustained-regime contrast, with 0011 versus 0101 worked out for L=2.

anchor_paper.author: Missault and Masoero
anchor_paper.claim: Influence-period misspecification can bias switchback estimators, but no deterministic nullspace alias is supplied.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample is stronger because it targets common alternating schedules and gives a nondegenerate nullspace direction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only linear algebra after writing the design matrix.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap context says the natural estimator-comparison route fails to expose exact policy-contrast null directions.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not MissaultMasoero2025 because it computes deterministic alias directions rather than testing lag underestimation.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the output is a certificate table and policy-relevant alias vector.

adversarial_referee.instance: L=2 witness might be too small.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It is the exhibit; the conjecture promotes the certificate to all L.
ExactID kernels are exempt from the Panel estimator-duality floor.
The proposal states it is a deterministic complement to their guessed-L problem.

seed_details.one_line: Separate classical residual balance from full L-history identification by constructing schedules that satisfy first-order crossover balance but have rank-deficient higher-order history incidence.

anchor_paper.author: Oman and Seiden
anchor_paper.claim: Switch-back and residual designs compare main and residual effects, not the full ordered history table.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: residual as well as main effects

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample is stronger if promoted from one schedule to a class of residual-balanced rank-deficient schedules.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is known in higher-order crossover design theory.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed would survive only by proving an explicit switchback history-incidence separation against named balance criteria.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not OmanSeiden1988 because residual-effect balance is not full primitive L-history exact identification.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the result gives an open class of rank-deficient residual-balanced schedules; otherwise downgrade.

adversarial_referee.instance: Higher-order designs may already close it.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs deeper crossover check before flagship use.
Promote to a family or downgrade.
Use as supporting theorem, not chosen headline.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize multi-unit switchback exact identification by the aggregate unit-period history-incidence matrix and determine when units can shorten each individual period below 2^L.

anchor_paper.author: Missault, Masoero, Delbe, Richardson, and Imbens
anchor_paper.claim: Regular balanced switchback designs randomize across time and items, but do not state the aggregate L-history rank frontier.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension from one unit to many is stronger only if it proves a unit-period tradeoff boundary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just stacking rows from the one-unit incidence matrix.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap says cyclic ordered-history constraints are not preserved by generic network-incidence stacking.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not MissaultMasoeroDelbeRichardsonImbens2025 because it asks for exact history-rank tradeoffs, not balanced estimation performance.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only with a matching or lower-bound object; not chosen for v1.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State full vector and contrast family jointly.
Keep it ExactID or add estimator.
Downgrade to M7 unless estimator added.

seed_details.one_line: Define the sustained-regime contrast row-space frontier: Delta_L is identified iff e_{1^L}-e_{0^L} lies in the row span of A(w,L), even when the full history vector is not identified.

anchor_paper.author: Bojinov, Simchi-Levi, and Zhao
anchor_paper.claim: Design and inference are optimized under carryover assumptions; contrast-specific row-space identification is not isolated.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation is stronger if contrast identification differs from full-vector identification in named schedules.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate linear functional identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: True; use as a theorem supporting the full frontier, not as flagship headline.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a de Bruijn-corrected switchback design audit that replaces alternating schedules when the alias vector changes the all-zero versus all-one policy contrast.

anchor_paper.author: Bojinov, Simchi-Levi, and Zhao
anchor_paper.claim: Switchback design recommendations are about optimality under assumed order, not alias-free full-history audits.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Decompose-then-correct is weaker unless the corrected design is an explicit certificate replacing a named schedule.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is not an estimator correction, only a design substitution.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; keep as applied corollary, not flagship theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Give a critical-lag certificate for influence-period misspecification: the first L at which a schedule loses full history-rank determines when guessed carryover bounds can bias sustained contrasts.

anchor_paper.author: Missault and Masoero
anchor_paper.claim: Testing underestimated carryover does not compute the exact schedule rank-loss boundary.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: detects when this bound is underestimated

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Critical-delta analogue is stronger if the critical lag is computable and changes contrast identification.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a repackaged rank calculation over L.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is useful but less sharp than the de Bruijn minimum-period frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Bridge geometric-mixing switchbacks and finite-memory switchbacks by proving when burn-in block designs induce a full-rank finite-history submatrix.

anchor_paper.author: Hu and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: Burn-in improves rates under geometric mixing, but does not target exact finite L-history coverage.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge is stronger only with a nontrivial equivalence between burn-in blocks and full-rank history incidence.
seed_details.referee_objection: Finite memory and geometric mixing are different models, so the bridge may be cosmetic.
seed_details.rebuttal: Use only after the core exact-ID frontier is accepted.

seed_details.one_line: Extend de Bruijn incidence to regular balanced multi-item switchbacks through a matching condition on item-time histories and a lower bound on total observed histories.

anchor_paper.author: Zeng et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Sequential rerandomization balances prognostic variables and first-order carryover blocks, not arbitrary L-history rank.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment variation is stronger only if matching constraints change the de Bruijn lower bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a many-unit version of the same matrix rank condition.
seed_details.rebuttal: The unit-period lower bound could be new, but it is not the cleanest first angle.

seed_details.one_line: Produce higher-order crossover alias witnesses showing that residual-effect balance can hold while primitive L-bit history means remain unidentified.

anchor_paper.author: Williams
anchor_paper.venue: Australian Journal of Scientific Research Series A
anchor_paper.claim: Classical residual-balance constructions do not equal full ordered-history causal identification.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample is weaker unless tied to a named residual-balanced construction and an open class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already standard higher-order carryover design theory.
seed_details.rebuttal: Likely too close to classical design; use as comparator material.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Bojinov, Simchi-Levi, and Zhao (Management Science 2023) derive optimal switchback designs under assumed carryover order and study misspecification, but optimize variance/inference rather than identify the full L-history mean table.
Missault and Masoero (2025) state that switchback estimators require an influence-period bound that is often guessed, motivating a deterministic schedule certificate.
Hu and Wager (2022/2025) study geometric mixing and show standard switchbacks can suffer carryover bias, adjacent to but distinct from finite-history exact identification.
Xiong, Chin, and Taylor (2024) decompose switchback error into carryover, periodicity, serial correlation, and simultaneous-experiment effects, but do not give a full history-incidence frontier.
Missault, Masoero, Delbe, Richardson, and Imbens (2025) and Zeng et al. (2026) push multi-unit balance and rerandomization, not arbitrary L-history incidence.
Williams (1949) and Oman-Seiden (1988) are the classical crossover/switch-back residual-balance comparators.
Aguirre, Mattar, and Magis-Weinberg (2011) use de Bruijn cycles for arbitrary stimulus-history counterbalance, so novelty cannot be mere use of de Bruijn sequences.
The live gap is a causal observation map from cyclic switchback treatment histories to all primitive L-history policy means, plus rank and alias certificates.
The strongest witness is L=2: 0011 gives histories 00,01,11,10 exactly once, while 0101 observes only 01 and 10 and admits delta=(1,0,-1,0).
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","mode":"revise","version":2,"message":"Revised the switchback proposal by demoting the already-known de Bruijn L=2 table to a sanity lemma, adding a finite schedule-audit certificate theorem, and replacing the definitional full-rank conjecture with an unknown-influence-period sustained-contrast frontier.","proposal_path":"CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/eid_switchback_debruijn_carryover/eid_switchback_debruijn_carryover_v1_proposal.tex","soundness_self_review":{"SC1_hypotheses_in_assumptions":"PASS - Theorem 1 cites finite-history through schedule-means assumptions; Theorem 2 cites finite-history through nonparametric assumptions; Conjecture 1 cites finite-history through schedule-means assumptions, all present in section 7.","SC2_objects_defined":"PASS - A(w,L), H_t, m(w,L), mu, theta_c, c_Delta, Delta_L, Theta_I, L_star(w,c), lambda, and delta are defined in sections 6-8 before use.","SC3_corollaries_reduce":"PASS - No-carryover corner collapses by identifying histories with common final treatment; known-L audit reduces to Theorem 2 for 0101 because the displayed alias has nonzero sustained-regime loading.","SC4_intro_kernel_matches_§8":"PASS - Section 4 lists Theorem 1 schedule-audit certificate, Theorem 2 alternating alias, and Conjecture 1 unknown-L frontier, matching section 8.","SC5_consumer_named":"PASS - Consumers are switchback estimator pre-analysis audits, MissaultMasoero2025 carryover-duration diagnostics, and MissaultMasoeroDelbeRichardsonImbens2025 robust switchback design.","SC6_comparator_promise":"PASS - BojinovSimchiLeviZhao2023 and MissaultMasoero2025 are matched by Theorem 1 and Conjecture 1; AguirreMattarMagisWeinberg2011 is downgraded to a compact-counterbalance witness, not the novelty claim; OmanSeiden1988 is matched by Theorem 2 alias separation.","SC7_no_tautological_iff":"PASS - The prior full-column-rank iff conjecture was removed as the flagship headline; the remaining iff in Theorem 1 is a finite certificate theorem and Conjecture 1 is framed as a target-specific validity frontier.","SC8_tuning_parameter_bounded":"PASS - The only frontier parameter is L_star(w,c), defined from primitive schedule histories and target row-span membership; no free epsilon or unbounded tuning parameter appears.","SC9_concrete_witness_exists":"PASS - Exhibit 9.1 gives concrete L=2 schedules 0011 and 0101, explicit matrices, and a nonzero alias vector changing Delta_2.","SC10_focal_object_exhibited":"Focal object: schedule-history incidence certificate A(w,2) and alias vector delta.\\nDefining equation (from section 6): A(w,L)_{t,h}=1{H_t(w,L)=h}, H_t(w,L)=(w_{t-L+1},...,w_t), and Delta_L=(e_{1^L}-e_{0^L})^T mu.\\nRHS symbol provenance:\\n- L=2; source: section 6 primitive fixed by locked parameters.\\n- w^D=0011 with cyclic indices 0,1,2,3; source: section 6 primitive schedule.\\n- w^A=0101 with cyclic indices 0,1,2,3; source: section 6 primitive schedule.\\n- H_2=(00,01,11,10); source: derived from section 6 primitive H_L={0,1}^L.\\n- delta=(1,0,-1,0)^T; source: derived null vector candidate from the section 6 incidence matrix.\\nSubstituted equation: for w^D=0011, (H_1,H_2,H_3,H_0)=(00,01,11,10), hence A(w^D,2)=I_4. For w^A=0101, (H_0,H_1,H_2,H_3)=(10,01,10,01), hence A(w^A,2) has rows e_10,e_01,e_10,e_01.\\nComputed value: A(w^D,2)=I_4, rank A(w^D,2)=4, A(w^A,2)delta=0, and (e_11-e_00)^T delta=-2.\\nInvariant check 1: each cyclic schedule has T=4 rows. LHS=4; RHS=4; result=OK.\\nInvariant check 2: every row has exactly one history label. For both displayed matrices, each row sum is 1; result=OK.\\nInvariant check 3: the de Bruijn table covers four distinct histories. LHS=4; RHS=2^2=4; result=OK.\\nInvariant check 4: the alias vector is observationally null but policy-relevant. LHS=A(w^A,2)delta=(0,0,0,0)^T; RHS=0; policy contrast shift=-2 != 0; result=OK.\\nAssumption-substitution screen: no RHS symbol above is sourced from section 7. The schedule, lag length, history set, and matrices are all computed from section 6 primitives. Load-bearing section 7 assumptions for flagship novelty: none.\\nSC10 verdict: PASS","SC11_proof_route_non_trivial":"Headline: Theorem 1 finite schedule-audit certificate.\\nStep 0a - unfold section 6 definitions: A(w,L), m(w,L), theta_c, and Theta_I(w,L,m); not counted in k.\\nStep 0b - apply section 7 assumptions: finite-history, cyclic-stationarity, consistency, positive-row, nonparametric, and schedule-means; not counted in k.\\nStep 1 (substantive): construct primal identifying equation A^T lambda=c, converting causal contrast identification into an observed schedule-mean functional.\\nStep 2 (substantive): apply finite row-space separation to produce delta in ker A when the primal equation is infeasible.\\nStep 3 (substantive): paste mu and mu+epsilon delta to prove observational equivalence with target disagreement over the nonparametric history class.\\nClassical result: finite-dimensional Fredholm alternative.\\nk=3; PASS iff k >= 3.\\nHeadline: Conjecture 1 unknown influence-period frontier.\\nStep 0a - unfold section 6 definitions: schedule-means information set and L_star(w,c_Delta); not counted in k.\\nStep 0b - apply section 7 assumptions: finite-history through schedule-means; not counted in k.\\nStep 1 (substantive): prove a nesting lemma for embedded sustained-regime contrasts as L increases.\\nStep 2 (substantive): characterize first validity loss by target-loading null vectors rather than full-vector rank.\\nStep 3 (substantive): prove uniform estimator invalidity through two-point alias experiments for each L>L_star(w,c_Delta).\\nStep 4 (substantive): separate all-contrast de Bruijn word coverage from target-specific sustained-contrast validity.\\nClassical result: finite matrix separation plus de Bruijn word coverage.\\nk=4; PASS iff k >= 3.\\nSC11 verdict: PASS"},"comparator_promise_table":[{"comparator_bibkey":"BojinovSimchiLeviZhao2023","comparator_claim":"Optimal switchback design and inference are developed under an assumed carryover order.","matched_by":"Theorem 1 and Conjecture 1","match_kind":"strict_tightening"},{"comparator_bibkey":"MissaultMasoero2025","comparator_claim":"Switchback estimators require a prespecified upper bound on carryover duration, often guessed before estimation.","matched_by":"Theorem 1 and Conjecture 1","match_kind":"strict_tightening"},{"comparator_bibkey":"AguirreMattarMagisWeinberg2011","comparator_claim":"De Bruijn cycles provide compact arbitrary-level counterbalanced stimulus sequences.","matched_by":"downgraded_to_informed_by","match_kind":"downgraded_to_informed_by"},{"comparator_bibkey":"OmanSeiden1988","comparator_claim":"Classical switch-back and residual-balance designs address direct and residual effects.","matched_by":"Theorem 2","match_kind":"non_representability"}],"novelty_justification":{"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue artefacts remain q1_spectral_phase_transition/p1_markov, eid_policy_commutator_phase/v1, and panel_approx_nbhd_interference/v1. banked_reason: Stopped early after D-0.5 angle 0 v1 was field-tier and v2 was rejected at letter tier for the same switchback schedule-history incidence object: de Bruijn counterbalance was already known, the L-star frontier was promissory/tautological, and the proof reduced to routine row-space/nullspace separation.
